description: Spaced-repetition flashcard deck. Tag each card with level + topic for adaptive review.

cards.back: Light (Jyoti) of God (Isha) — the Vedic science of the celestial lights.
Siddhanta (astronomy/math), Samhita (mundane), Hora (predictive/natal).
Jataka, Prasna, Muhurta, Nimitta, Ganita, Gola.
Siksha, Kalpa, Vyakarana, Nirukta, Chhanda, Jyotisha.

cards.front: List the 5 core building blocks of Jyotish.
cards.back: 9 Grahas, 12 Rasis, 12 Bhavas, 27 Nakshatras, Karakatwas.

cards.front: Who authored Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra?
cards.back: Rishi Parashara (foundational classical text of Jyotish).
Interpretation vs Observation.
Rigveda (composed ~1500–1200 BCE, 1,028 suktas in 10 mandalas).
An imaginary sphere at infinite distance concurrent with Earth; the backdrop for mapping celestial objects.
The apparent path of the Sun across the sky over a year; Earth's orbital plane.
Zenith = directly overhead (MC). Nadir = directly below (IC). 180° apart.

cards.front: Tropical vs Sidereal zodiac — which does Jyotish use?
cards.back: Sidereal (Nirayana) — fixed to stars, Ashwini = 0° Aries. Tropical is Sayana, season-based.
The angular difference between Tropical and Sidereal zodiacs. Currently ~24°. Grows ~1° every 72 years.

cards.front: Why are Rahu and Ketu always retrograde?
cards.back: They are mathematical points (Moon's nodes), not bodies. They move backward against the zodiac at ~1 sign per 18 months.
'Shining wheel' — the zodiac belt. Bha = shine, Chakra = wheel.

cards.front: How large is the Zodiac belt?
cards.back: 360° around; ~8° north-south of the ecliptic.
Uttarayana = Sun's northward journey (Dec–June). Dakshinayana = southward (June–Dec).

cards.front: Why doesn't Jyotish use Neptune and Uranus?
cards.back: They are too slow-moving and their tidal force on Earth is negligible.

cards.front: How many degrees does one nakshatra span?

cards.back: 108 padas. 27 nakshatras × 4 padas each.
9 padas per sign. (2¼ nakshatras fit per sign.)
Ketu → Venus → Sun → Moon → Mars → Rahu → Jupiter → Saturn → Mercury.
Ketu. (Ashwini is the 1st nakshatra; the cycle starts with Ketu.)
Moon. (4th in the cycle: Ketu, Venus, Sun, Moon.)
The nakshatra occupied by the Moon at birth. Used for naming, Dasha start, and Tarabalam.
Janma, Sampat, Vipat, Kshema, Pratyak, Sadhaka, Naidhana, Mitra, Adhi-Mitra.

cards.front: What are the 3 Ganas of Nakshatras?
cards.back: Deva (divine, optimistic), Manushya (human, pragmatic), Rakshasa (demonic, rule-breaking).

cards.front: Which 2 nakshatras are Misra (mix of soft + sharp)?
cards.back: Krittika and Vishakha — both under Agni.
Dharma → Artha → Kama → Moksha, repeating. Pada 1 = Dharma, Pada 4 = Moksha.
Cycle. Refers to each of the 3 groups of 9 nakshatras (Ashwini–Ashlesha, Magha–Jyeshtha, Moola–Revati).
Self, soul, father, power, government, vitality, heart, bones.
Mind, mother, emotions, public, water, home, breast/lungs/stomach.
Energy, action, siblings, war, land, surgery, muscles, bone marrow.
Intelligence, communication, speech, business, nervous system, skin.
Wisdom, wealth, children, religion, spiritual guru, liver, fat.
Love, marriage, women, arts, beauty, vehicles, reproductive system.
Discipline, longevity, old people, service, struggle, bones/teeth, delay.
Illusion, obsession, foreign things, occult, addiction, sudden fame.
Detachment, moksha, mysticism, healing, isolation, siddhas.
Exalted in Aries 10°, debilitated in Libra 10°.
Exalted in Taurus 3°, debilitated in Scorpio 3°.
Exalted in Capricorn 28°, debilitated in Cancer 28°.
Exalted in Virgo 15°, debilitated in Pisces 15°.
Exalted in Cancer 5°, debilitated in Capricorn 5°.
Exalted in Pisces 27°, debilitated in Virgo 27°.
Exalted in Libra 20°, debilitated in Aries 20°.
Rahu exalted in Taurus 20° (some say Gemini), Ketu in Scorpio 20°. Each debilitated in opposite.
4th, 7th, 8th houses from its position.
5th, 7th, 9th houses.
3rd, 7th, 10th houses.
Waxing Moon, Mercury (alone or with benefics), Jupiter, Venus.
Sun, Saturn, Mars, Waning Moon, Rahu, Ketu.
Sagittarius 0°–10°.
Aquarius 0°–20°.

cards.front: Dig Bala — which planets strong in 1st house?
cards.back: Mercury and Jupiter (East).
Sun and Mars (South, midday strength).
Moon and Venus (North, night sky luminaries).
Saturn (West, setting sun).
A planet that lords BOTH a Kendra AND a Trikona for a given Lagna. Gives Raja Yoga.
Buddhi-Aditya Yoga — sharp intellect and communication.
Gajakesari Yoga — wisdom, prosperity, respect.
Mars / Fire / Cardinal / Dharma. Body: head.
Venus / Earth / Fixed / Artha. Body: face, neck.
Mercury / Air / Mutable / Kama. Body: arms, shoulders.
Moon / Water / Cardinal / Moksha. Body: heart, chest.
Sun / Fire / Fixed / Dharma. Body: stomach.
Mercury / Earth / Mutable / Artha. Body: waist, intestines.
Venus / Air / Cardinal / Kama. Body: lower abdomen.
Mars / Water / Fixed / Moksha. Body: genitals, hidden parts.
Jupiter / Fire / Mutable / Dharma. Body: thighs.
Saturn / Earth / Cardinal / Artha. Body: knees.
Saturn / Air / Fixed / Kama. Body: calves.
Jupiter / Water / Mutable / Moksha. Body: feet.
Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn.
Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius.
Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces.
Aries, Leo, Sagittarius.
Taurus, Virgo, Capricorn.
Gemini, Libra, Aquarius.
Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces.
Aries, Leo, Sagittarius (fire signs).
Cancer, Scorpio, Pisces (water).
Aries, Gemini, Leo, Virgo.
Gemini, Leo, Virgo, Libra, Scorpio, Aquarius. Strong in daytime.

cards.front: Which is the only Ubhayodaya (both-way rising) sign?
cards.back: Pisces.
Fire=Pitta, Earth=Vata, Water=Kapha, Air=Mixed.
Tanu Bhava — Self, body, personality, head. Natural ruler: Mars (Aries).
Dhana Bhava — Wealth, family, speech. Natural ruler: Venus (Taurus). Karaka: Jupiter.
Sahaja Bhava — Siblings, courage, communication. Natural ruler: Mercury. Karaka: Mars.
Bandhu/Sukha Bhava — Mother, home, happiness. Natural ruler: Moon. Karaka: Moon + Mercury.
Putra Bhava — Children, wisdom, Poorva Punya. Natural ruler: Sun. Karaka: Jupiter.
Ari/Ripu Bhava — Enemies, diseases, debts, service. Natural ruler: Mercury. Karakas: Mars, Saturn.
Yuvati/Kalatra Bhava — Spouse, partnerships. Natural ruler: Venus. Karaka: Venus.
Randhra Bhava — Longevity, occult, transformation. Natural ruler: Mars. Karaka: Saturn.
Dharma Bhava — Father, fortune, religion. Natural ruler: Jupiter. Karaka: Sun + Jupiter.
Karma Bhava — Career, status. Natural ruler: Saturn. Karakas: Sun, Jupiter, Mercury, Saturn.
Labha Bhava — Gains, friends, wishes. Natural ruler: Saturn. Karaka: Jupiter.
Vyaya Bhava — Losses, liberation, foreign. Natural ruler: Jupiter. Karaka: Saturn.
1, 4, 7, 10 — Angular/Vishnu Sthana. Most powerful houses.
1, 5, 9 — Trines/Lakshmi Sthana. Most auspicious.
3, 6, 10, 11 — Growth houses. Malefics perform well.
6, 8, 12 — Challenging. Generally avoid benefics here.
2, 7 — death-inflicting (12th from 3rd and 8th, the Jeeva Shakti houses).
Saturn — rules 9th and 10th.
Mars — rules 5th and 10th.
Saturn — rules 4th and 5th.
Venus — rules 4th and 9th.
11th house.
9th house.
7th house.
Examining a house by counting the same relative position from it. E.g. 5th from 5th = 9th.

cards.front: First 4 steps to analyse a house?
cards.back: 1. House Rasi, 2. House Lord, 3. Occupants, 4. Aspects.

cards.front: What is the division size of Navamsa?
cards.back: 3°20' (1/9 of a 30° sign). Same as one nakshatra pada.

cards.front: D9 — fire signs start navamsa from?
cards.back: Aries. (Aries, Leo, Sagittarius all start from Aries.)
Capricorn.
Libra.
Cancer.
When a planet or Lagna sits in the same sign in both D1 (Rasi) and D9 (Navamsa). Extra strength.
Spouse/marriage analysis. Also confirms D1 planetary strength and shows dharmic path.
The sign in D9 occupied by the Atmakaraka (highest-longitude planet). The 12th from it shows Ishta Devata.
0°00' – 3°20' (1st amsa). Cardinal = Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn.
